_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-18 17:14:42Z)**_

**Findings**
- High: `packages/rich-renderer-image/src/ImageEditRenderer.tsx:85` writes `trimmedSrc` directly into `writable.__src` in `commitChanges` without applying the same sanitization used on image-node creation. `packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/ImageNode.ts:31` applies `sanitizeImageSrc`, so edit flow and insert flow now differ. This can allow unsafe schemes (for example `javascript:` or invalid `data:` payloads) to persist after edit. Use shared sanitization before mutating node state.
- Medium: `packages/rich-renderer-image/src/ImageEditRenderer.tsx:1` introduces a new interaction-heavy edit UI (`open`, `commitChanges`, `handleDelete`, focus/reset behavior) with no accompanying test coverage. This raises regression risk around commit/delete/state transitions and parity with existing renderer patterns.

**Open question**
- Should `sanitizeImageSrc` be exported from `@shiro/rich-editor` (or moved to shared utility) so both node creation and edit updates use one canonical rule?

**Scope reviewed**
- `packages/rich-renderer-image/package.json`
- `packages/rich-renderer-image/src/index.ts`
- `packages/rich-renderer-image/src/ImageEditRenderer.tsx`
- `packages/rich-renderer-image/src/styles.css.ts`
- `packages/rich-renderer-image/vite.config.ts`
- `packages/rich-renderers-edit/package.json`
- `packages/rich-renderers-edit/src/config.ts`
- `packages/rich-renderers-edit/src/index.ts`
- `pnpm-lock.yaml`
- `plans/polymorphic-sniffing-stonebraker.md`
